extraction.one_line_summary: NIBALI Vincenzo held off a rapidly closing GC group for a solo stage win after escaping early, while ALAPHILIPPE cracked on the final climb losing the podium to MAS Enric.
tactical_patterns: NIBALI exploited early break from short stage format, holding 39 seconds with 1.4km remaining despite solo effort on brutal climb.
ALAPHILIPPE cracked mid-climb forcing MAS to wait, showing top favorites attack when they detect weakness in sustained efforts not initial accelerations.
MÜHLBERGER paced BUCHMANN aggressively in closing kilometers but waited too long to launch, missing 39-second gap window for stage contention.
THOMAS sacrificed stage placing to pace BERNAL, revealing team willingness to surrender individual results when GC already secured pre-final stage.

rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: positive: Won solo despite visible suffering last 250m, showing he can time efforts to hold minimal gaps under extreme fatigue
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: Lost podium by cracking mid-climb on steep gradients, confirming vulnerability when sustained pressure applied rather than explosive attacks
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: Secured yellow jersey day before Paris while teammate THOMAS paced him, demonstrating team control without needing personal攻击
rider_signals.MAS Enric: neutral: Waited for cracking ALAPHILIPPE mid-climb but still couldn't hold podium position, questionable team tactics cost time
rider_signals.MÜHLBERGER Gregor: positive: Paced BUCHMANN deep into final climb reducing GC group to 12 riders, exceptional domestique work at altitude
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: neutral: Appeared in break but commentary unclear if riding for self or domestique for URÁN, ambiguous role cost opportunities
rider_signals.BUCHMANN Emanuel: negative: Had MÜHLBERGER pacing late but finished 7th, missed opportunity when teammate delivered him perfectly positioned
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: Second place at age 39 on brutal final Alpine stage shows world champion form holds on sustained climbs
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: Third place finish 14 seconds back demonstrates consistency on long climbs when not required to initiate attacks
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Sacrificed stage result to pace BERNAL showing defending champion professionalism in team leadership transition
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: negative: Dropped early when NIBALI increased tempo in breakaway, weakness revealed before reaching steepest gradients
